WARNING: CTO variant (820-02443). SVR AON is 42A design. Viper has 3 stage pages. Monaco has 5 instances (C0-C4). Page numbers differ from base.
description: Boot power-on sequence for M1 Pro/Max CTO. Key CTO differences: 42A SVR AON, 3 Viper stage pages, 5 Monaco instances for extended Max GPU phases.

sequence.name: USB-C Cable Insertion — 5V Default
sequence.action: USB-C cable inserted. Charger provides default 5V. CD3217 (UF400/UF500/UG400) internal MOSFET passes 5V.

sequence.name: Charger Input Active — PPDCIN_AON
sequence.action: Charger input path enabled. Multi-FET gate drive active. CHGR_AUX_OK asserted.

sequence.name: PPBUS_AON Established
sequence.action: Main power bus comes up from battery (12.6V) or adapter input.

sequence.name: PP3V8_AON Powers Up — U5700 SVR AON (42A CTO)
sequence.action: U5700 converts PPBUS_AON -> PP3V8_AON (3.8V, 42A max CTO). Multi-phase output with 3-phase summing amp ISENSE/ILIMIT. Feeds both PMUs + WiFi + display + USB-C.
sequence.cto_difference: 42A design (base: 30A). BOM options: BOM0=30A, BOM1=35A, BOM2=42A

sequence.name: SPMU & MPMU Startup — AON and S2 Rails Cascade
sequence.action: Both PMUs receive PP3V8_AON and generate output rails in hardware-defined sequence. AON rails first, then S2, then S1.

sequence.name: Standalone VRs Power Up — 5V and 3V3
sequence.action: TPS62130 generates PP5V_S2, separate VR generates PP3V3_S2. Load switches enabled for USB-C ports and HDMI.

sequence.name: Viper VR Powers Up — PCPU Domains (3 Stage Pages CTO)
sequence.action: U9300 Viper VR controller starts multi-phase power stages. CTO has 3 stage pages (49-51) vs base 2. PCPU clusters receive DVFS voltage.
sequence.cto_difference: 3 power stage pages for additional Max CPU phases

sequence.name: Monaco VR Powers Up — GPU/Fabric Domains (5 Instances CTO)
sequence.action: Monaco VR instances start. CTO has 5 instances (C0-C4) vs base 3 (C0, C1, C4). Monaco 2 (C2) and Monaco 3 (C3) are CTO-only for extended Max GPU phases. 10 bucks per instance.
sequence.cto_difference: 5 Monaco instances (C0-C4). C2 and C3 add Max GPU power phases.

sequence.name: M1 Pro/Max Hard Start Complete — SoC Commands CD3217
sequence.action: SoC completed OTP and hard start. Commands CD3217 to negotiate PD.

sequence.name: PD Negotiation -> 20V Boost
sequence.action: CD3217 negotiates 20V from charger. PPVBUS transitions 5V -> 20V. Protection isolation passes. PPBUS_AON stabilises.

sequence.name: Soft Start — SSD, BIOS, Display
sequence.action: SoC reads dual SSDs (SSD0 + SSD1 via Ocarina), loads firmware, display power sequencer enables Mini-LED Liquid Retina XDR panel, Luxe backlight driver activates. Apple LOGO appears.
subsystems: HDMI (Madea, page 93) — if external display connected
